syn_cs_yorel_heskenwald: Yorel Heskenwald is a computer scientist at ETH Zurich, where he holds a faculty appointment in the Department of Computer Science. His research focuses on programming language theory, with emphasis on dependent type systems, effect systems, and the metatheory of gradually typed languages. He has published in venues such as POPL, ICFP, and OOPSLA, and is a co-designer of a research language used internally to study modular soundness proofs. Before joining ETH Zurich, he completed his doctorate on coeffect calculi and held a postdoctoral position in northern Europe. He teaches graduate courses on type theory and program logics and supervises several PhD students working on verified compilation and refinement types.
syn_cs_anveer_kothakonda: Anveer Kothakonda is a computer scientist at the University of Edinburgh, where he is a faculty member in the School of Informatics. His research is in systems for machine learning, including distributed training schedulers, memory-efficient checkpointing, and serving infrastructure for large language models. He has published at venues such as OSDI, SOSP, MLSys, and NSDI, and has collaborated with industry labs on production-scale training systems. Prior to Edinburgh he completed his PhD at a North American university and worked briefly in industry on cluster scheduling. He teaches operating systems and a graduate course on ML systems, and his group maintains an open-source profiling toolkit used by several academic collaborators.
syn_cs_eskil_vrancken: Eskil Vrancken is a computer scientist at KU Leuven, where he holds a faculty position in the Department of Computer Science. His research is in formal verification, focusing on interactive theorem proving, separation logic, and the verification of concurrent data structures. He has published at venues including POPL, PLDI, CAV, and ITP, and is a contributor to a widely used proof assistant library for concurrent program logics. Before KU Leuven he completed his doctorate at a Dutch university and held a postdoctoral fellowship in Germany. He teaches courses on logic, semantics, and verification, and supervises PhD students working on machine-checked proofs of distributed protocols.
syn_cs_vihaan_periswamy: Vihaan Periswamy is a computer scientist at the Indian Institute of Science in Bangalore, where he holds a faculty appointment in the Department of Computational and Data Sciences. His research is on database systems, with emphasis on query optimization, learned index structures, and storage engines for hybrid transactional/analytical workloads. He has published at venues such as SIGMOD, VLDB, and ICDE, and his group has released an open-source benchmark suite for evaluating learned database components. He completed his PhD in North America, returned to India to take up a faculty role, and teaches courses on database internals and data-intensive systems.
syn_cs_tarvik_olshanski: Tarvik Olshanski is a computer scientist at the University of Toronto, where he holds a faculty position in the Department of Computer Science. His research is in theoretical cryptography, including lattice-based assumptions, succinct arguments, and zero-knowledge proof systems. He has published at venues such as CRYPTO, EUROCRYPT, TCC, and STOC, and has co-authored papers on post-quantum signature schemes. Before Toronto he was a postdoctoral researcher at an East-Coast U.S. university, and completed his PhD in Europe. He teaches graduate courses on modern cryptography and complexity theory, and supervises students working on succinct non-interactive arguments of knowledge (SNARKs).
syn_cs_onnele_sjoberg: Onnele Sjöberg is a computer scientist at KTH Royal Institute of Technology in Stockholm, where she holds a faculty appointment in the School of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science. Her research is on distributed systems, focusing on consensus protocols, geo-replicated storage, and the formal modelling of Byzantine fault tolerance. She has published at venues such as NSDI, OSDI, EuroSys, and PODC. Before KTH she completed her PhD in Finland and held a postdoctoral position in Switzerland. She teaches an undergraduate operating-systems course and a graduate seminar on distributed-systems verification, and supervises students working on practical BFT protocols for blockchain settings.
syn_cs_devansh_maliholkar: Devansh Maliholkar is a computer scientist at Georgia Tech, where he holds a faculty appointment in the School of Interactive Computing. His research is in natural language processing, focusing on dialogue systems, instruction-following, and faithfulness of model-generated explanations. He has published at venues such as ACL, EMNLP, NAACL, and TACL, and has collaborated with industry labs on instruction-tuned models. Before Georgia Tech he completed his PhD at a U.S. university and was briefly a research scientist at an industrial lab. He teaches an undergraduate NLP course and a graduate seminar on language-model evaluation, and his group releases evaluation datasets for grounded dialogue.
syn_cs_wenslin_kraborsky: Wenslin Kraborsky is a computer scientist at the Technical University of Munich, where he holds a faculty position in the Department of Computer Science. His research is on robotics and reinforcement learning, focusing on sample-efficient policy learning, sim-to-real transfer, and dexterous manipulation. He has published at venues such as NeurIPS, ICRA, CoRL, and RSS, and his lab maintains an open-source simulation environment used by collaborators in Germany and abroad. Before TUM he completed his PhD in North America and held an industrial research position. He teaches a graduate course on reinforcement learning for robotics and supervises PhD students working on tactile sensing and contact-rich manipulation.
syn_cs_halvar_mendigutia: Halvar Mendigutia is a computer scientist at the University of Helsinki, where he holds a faculty appointment in the Department of Computer Science. His research is in computer vision, with emphasis on self-supervised representation learning, dense prediction tasks, and multimodal alignment between vision and language. He has published at venues such as CVPR, ICCV, ECCV, and NeurIPS. Before Helsinki he completed his PhD at a Nordic university and held a postdoctoral fellowship in the United Kingdom. He teaches an undergraduate computer-vision course and a graduate seminar on self-supervised learning, and his group has released open-source pretraining code used by external collaborators.
syn_cs_iskander_vrolijk: Iskander Vrolijk is a computer scientist at Delft University of Technology, where he holds a faculty appointment in the Faculty of Electrical Engineering, Mathematics and Computer Science. His research is on graph neural networks, with emphasis on expressivity, scalable training on large graphs, and applications to molecular property prediction. He has published at venues such as NeurIPS, ICML, ICLR, and KDD. Before TU Delft he completed his PhD at a European university and held a postdoctoral position in North America. He teaches a graduate course on geometric deep learning and supervises students working on the theoretical foundations of message-passing networks.
syn_founder_brendel_quanzhong: Brendel Quanzhong is the co-founder and CEO of an AI infrastructure startup founded in 2023, focused on distributed training of large foundation models. The company builds a software stack for elastic, fault-tolerant training on heterogeneous GPU clusters, with customers among AI labs and frontier research groups. Before founding the company he was an engineering lead at a major cloud provider, working on collective communication libraries and large-scale training jobs. He holds a graduate degree in computer science and has co-authored several systems papers on distributed deep learning. The company raised a seed round shortly after incorporation and a Series A from a top-tier venture firm in 2024.
syn_founder_marielys_caballenas: Marielys Caballenas is the co-founder and CEO of a developer-tools startup founded in 2022, focused on observability for modern cloud-native applications. The company builds an open-source instrumentation framework and a SaaS backend that ingests traces, metrics, and logs in a unified format, with customers among mid-market SaaS companies. Before founding the company she was a staff engineer at a large observability vendor and earlier worked on site reliability engineering at a consumer-internet company. She is a frequent speaker at developer conferences and the company has raised a seed and Series A round backed by enterprise-software investors.
syn_founder_jocelin_okwuosu: Jocelin Okwuosu is the co-founder and CTO of an AI agents startup founded in 2024, focused on enterprise workflow automation. The company builds long-running autonomous agents that interact with internal SaaS tools to handle back-office tasks such as procurement, expense reconciliation, and HR onboarding, with early enterprise customers in financial services. Before founding the company she was a senior research engineer at an industrial AI lab, working on tool-using agents and on the post-training of dialogue models. She holds a PhD in computer science and has published on language-model agents. The company closed a substantial seed round in 2024.
syn_founder_pieter_van_houtrijn: Pieter van Houtrijn is the co-founder and CEO of a robotics startup founded in 2021, building autonomous warehouse systems. The company sells mobile manipulation robots and a fleet-orchestration platform to third-party logistics providers, replacing manual picking and putaway in mid-size warehouses. Before founding the company he led an autonomy team at a European automotive supplier and earlier worked on industrial robotics at a major Japanese manufacturer. He holds a master's degree in mechanical engineering. The company has raised Series A and Series B funding from industrial and venture investors, and has commercial deployments in the Netherlands, Germany, and the United States.
syn_founder_renske_thalvald: Renske Thalvald is the co-founder and CTO of a cybersecurity startup founded in 2023, focused on supply-chain attack detection. The company builds a platform that monitors third-party software dependencies, container images, and CI/CD pipelines for tampering and malicious updates, serving enterprise security teams primarily in Europe. Before founding the company she was a principal security engineer at a large cloud provider, working on software supply-chain integrity and code-signing infrastructure. She has spoken at industry conferences on reproducible builds and SBOM tooling. The company has raised seed and Series A funding led by enterprise-security investors.
syn_founder_amitabh_nakshatri: Amitabh Nakshatri is the co-founder and CEO of a vector-database startup founded in 2022, serving retrieval-augmented generation workloads. The company builds a managed vector store optimized for hybrid lexical and dense retrieval, with primitives for incremental indexing, filtered search, and access control, used by enterprise customers building internal copilots. Before founding the company he was an engineering manager at a major search vendor and earlier worked on relevance ranking at a large e-commerce company. He holds a graduate degree in computer science. The company has raised seed and Series A rounds from infrastructure-focused venture firms.
syn_imo_vesper_lindblom: Vesper Lindblom won gold at the International Mathematical Olympiad in 2014, representing Sweden. IMO gold medalists are the top ~50 high-school mathematics competitors worldwide each year. Many IMO medalists go on to careers in academic mathematics, computer science, finance, or technology; a substantial fraction obtain mathematics PhDs at top research universities.
syn_imo_aslan_shyngyssov: Aslan Shyngyssov won gold at the International Mathematical Olympiad in 2014, representing Kazakhstan. IMO gold medalists are the top ~50 high-school mathematics competitors worldwide each year. Many IMO medalists go on to careers in academic mathematics, computer science, finance, or technology; a substantial fraction obtain mathematics PhDs at top research universities.
syn_imo_petr_zelinka: Petr Zelinka won gold at the International Mathematical Olympiad in 2014, representing Czechia. IMO gold medalists are the top ~50 high-school mathematics competitors worldwide each year. Many IMO medalists go on to careers in academic mathematics, computer science, finance, or technology; a substantial fraction obtain mathematics PhDs at top research universities.
syn_imo_quoc_nhat_dau: Quốc Nhật Đậu won gold at the International Mathematical Olympiad in 2014, representing Vietnam. IMO gold medalists are the top ~50 high-school mathematics competitors worldwide each year. Many IMO medalists go on to careers in academic mathematics, computer science, finance, or technology; a substantial fraction obtain mathematics PhDs at top research universities.
syn_imo_milos_pravdic: Miloš Pravdić won gold at the International Mathematical Olympiad in 2014, representing Serbia. IMO gold medalists are the top ~50 high-school mathematics competitors worldwide each year. Many IMO medalists go on to careers in academic mathematics, computer science, finance, or technology; a substantial fraction obtain mathematics PhDs at top research universities.
syn_imo_zhuoming_qiang: Zhuoming Qiang won gold at the International Mathematical Olympiad in 2014, representing China. IMO gold medalists are the top ~50 high-school mathematics competitors worldwide each year. Many IMO medalists go on to careers in academic mathematics, computer science, finance, or technology; a substantial fraction obtain mathematics PhDs at top research universities.
syn_oss_spindleflow: Spindleflow is an open-source workflow orchestration library for Python data pipelines, hosted at github.com/spindleflow/spindleflow. It provides a declarative DAG API for defining batch and streaming workflows, with built-in operators for cloud object storage, SQL warehouses, and container runtimes. Spindleflow includes a scheduler, a web UI for monitoring runs, and integration with common observability backends. The project targets analytics engineering and ML data preparation use cases, positioned between lightweight task runners and large enterprise orchestrators. It is released under the Apache 2.0 license and maintained by a small group of contributors with a public roadmap on GitHub.
syn_oss_quillvec: Quillvec is an open-source vector indexing library optimized for low-latency similarity search, hosted at github.com/quillvec/quillvec. It provides on-disk indexes for high-dimensional embeddings using a quantized graph-based algorithm, with bindings for Python, Rust, and C++. Quillvec is designed for retrieval-augmented generation use cases that require sub-millisecond search over millions of vectors with metadata filters. It is released under the Apache 2.0 license, with a small core team of maintainers and external contributors adding language bindings and integrations.
syn_oss_lattispan: Lattispan is an open-source distributed-tracing library for asynchronous Python services, hosted at github.com/lattispan/lattispan. It provides OpenTelemetry-compatible context propagation across asyncio tasks, threads, and subprocesses, with low-overhead sampling and structured-log correlation. The library is designed for high-throughput web services, queue workers, and ML serving stacks where standard tracing instrumentation tends to lose context across async boundaries. Lattispan is released under the Apache 2.0 license and maintained by a small group of contributors, with documentation and examples hosted alongside the GitHub repository.
syn_oss_glaivebench: Glaivebench is an open-source benchmark suite for evaluating long-context reasoning in language models. It provides task families that probe multi-document synthesis, long-range coreference, and needle-in-a-haystack retrieval at context lengths from 32k to 1M tokens, along with a harness for running closed and open models against the suite. Glaivebench includes baseline numbers for several frontier models and a public leaderboard. The project is released under a permissive license and is maintained by a small group of academic and independent contributors, with new task families added on a rolling basis.
syn_mid_musician_bruno_esquivel_tang: Bruno Esquivel-Tang is a Mexican indie folk singer-songwriter based in Mexico City. He released his debut album Marisma Azul in 2022 to favourable reviews from Latin American music press, blending acoustic guitar arrangements with electronic textures and bilingual lyrics. He has toured Mexico, Spain, and the southern United States as a supporting act for better-known indie artists and has played festival slots at mid-size venues. His second album was released in 2024 and his work has been featured on curated playlists at major streaming services. He is not yet widely known outside the Spanish-language indie circuit.
syn_mid_chef_celestin_rouvenaz: Célestin Rouvenaz is a Swiss chef and the owner of Le Tournesol Bleu, a restaurant in Vevey on the shores of Lake Geneva. The restaurant earned its first Michelin star in 2023 and is known for a tasting menu that pairs alpine produce with techniques drawn from French and northern Italian cuisine. Rouvenaz trained in Lyon and worked at several starred kitchens in Switzerland and France before opening his own restaurant in 2019. He is a regular contributor to Swiss culinary publications and has appeared on regional food television.
syn_mid_journalist_mireia_aldecoa: Mireia Aldecoa is a Spanish foreign-affairs correspondent for the online newspaper elDiario.es, covering Latin American politics from a base in Madrid with extended reporting trips across the region. Her recent work has focused on electoral cycles in the Andean countries, judicial-reform debates in Mexico, and the politics of migration along the Darién Gap. She previously worked at a Spanish news agency as a regional correspondent in South America and her reporting has been republished by several European newspapers. She holds a degree in journalism from a Spanish university.
syn_mid_podcast_the_quiet_signal: The Quiet Signal is a long-form documentary podcast about underreported scientific controversies, launched in 2021. Each season focuses on a single topic, with episodes drawing on archival audio, original interviews with researchers and patients, and on-location reporting. Past seasons have covered disputed clinical trials, replication failures in social science, and contested questions in environmental epidemiology. The show is produced by an independent team of two reporters and a sound designer, distributed via major podcast platforms, and supported by listener donations and a small number of sponsors.
